patterns.alias: The shorthand or alternate name commonly used to refer to this layer (e.g. "Silver" or "Designed"). Helps map your naming to other names or architecture languages.
patterns.layer-governance-policy: Is the use of this Data Layer Enforced and must be used, is it Advised and should it be used or is the use of the layer Optional.
patterns.contains-pii: Indicates whether this layer includes Personally Identifiable Information and may require additional governance, security, and privacy controls.
patterns.concept-modeling-patterns: The Conceptual modeling patterns used to design the data in this layer.
patterns.data-persistence: Whether data in this layer is temporary, cached, or permanently stored.
patterns.data-storage-technology: The underlying platform or technology used to store data in this layer.
patterns.data-storage-format: The technical file or table format used to persist data.
patterns.default-retention-period: The standard timeframe data in this layer is kept before being archived or deleted.
patterns.physical-data-structure: The Physical modeling patterns used to store data in this layer.
patterns.source-driven: Whether the layer is designed primarily by the structure of the source system, rather than business concepts / business models.
patterns.integrates-core-business-concepts: Whether data in this layer is aligned to and integrates the organisation's core business concepts (customers, products, orders, etc.).
patterns.data-processing: The cadence and pattern by which data is processed, transformed and made available (batch, micro-batch, streaming, real-time).
patterns.change-detection: The Patterns used to identify and process changes in the data.
patterns.destructive-rebuilds: Are Destructive Rebuilds of the data allowed in this layer.
patterns.rename-fields: Are Fields allowed to be renamed differently to the Fields in the previous layer and if so for what reason.
patterns.data-value-updates: Can data values in this layer be UPDATED in place, or data is treated as immutable and only INSERTS are allowed.
patterns.derived-data: Does this layer contain NEW DATA ELEMENTS created through transformations, derivations, or enrichments, or does it only hold data from the previous layer.
patterns.calculate-metrics: Are business metrics calculated and stored in this layer.
patterns.auditable: Can the layer be audited and reconciled with another layer or the system of capture.
patterns.reconciliation-pattern: What Data Engineering Patterns are used to reconcile the data in this layer.
patterns.data-contract-alerts: Are notifications triggered when incoming data violates agreed-upon data contracts.
patterns.data-quality-alerts: Are notifications triggered when incoming data violates defined data-quality rules.
patterns.persona-access-read: Which Personas have permission to READ the data in this layer.
patterns.persona-access-write: Which Personas have permission to WRITE the data in this layer.
patterns.last-mile-tools: What Last Mile tools are used to access the data in the layers.
patterns.history-window: The timeframe of data each delivery / table / file is expected to cover — either full history or a rolling window (e.g. 7 days, 90 days, 1 year).
patterns.status: Lifecycle stage of the data contract / artefact. Aligned with the OpenMetadata Standards 7-stage lifecycle: ideation → design → development → testing → production → deprecated → retired.

alias.Atomic: Data stored at the most granular level, often unaggregated and unmodified. Ideal for reconstructing events or applying different transformations later.
alias.Base: A foundational layer where ingested data is lightly processed but still closely mirrors the source. Often used as a starting point for downstream pipelines.
alias.Integrated: Data that has been joined across systems and aligned on shared keys or entities. Often the equivalent of the "Design" layer.
alias.Intermediate: A temporary or working layer used to support transformation steps between source and target layers.
alias.Kitchen: A metaphorical layer name based on a Restaurant. An internal, messy layer where data is transformed, cooked, and prepared before being served. Not suitable for consumption. Often the equivalent to the "Design" layer.
alias.Landing: Where data lands immediately after data collection/ingestion, typically before any processing or transformation is applied.
alias.Loading Dock: A metaphorical layer name based on a Restaurant or Shop. Similar to Landing, temporarily holding raw data before loading into a layer where data is persisted. Often the equivalent to the "Landing" layer.
alias.Marts: An alias for "Data Marts", subject-specific datasets designed for reporting and analysis (e.g. Sales Mart, Finance Mart). Usually derived and summarised. Often the equivalent to the "Consume" layer.
alias.Metrics Layer: A layer where key metrics and KPIs are centrally defined and calculated. Often used in semantic or headless BI tools. Often the equivalent of the "Consume" layer.
alias.Persistent Staging Area: A layer where raw data is staged and stored persistently (not just temporarily), often for audit or recovery purposes. Often the equivalent of the "Consume" layer.
alias.Platinum: An extended metaphor beyond Gold, implies an even higher level of trust, quality, or certification. Often the equivalent of the "Consume" layer.
alias.Presentation: A final layer optimised for consumption. Often includes flattening, aliasing, and aggregation for tools like dashboards or APIs. Often the equivalent of the "Consume" layer.
alias.Raw: Untouched, untransformed data as landed from source systems. Typically include source schema and original naming. Often the equivalent of the "History" layer.
alias.Refined: Data that's been cleaned, transformed, and aligned, one step before being trusted or curated. Often the equivalent of the "Consume" layer.
alias.Restaurant: A metaphorical layer name based on a Restaurant, representing curated, served data, ready for users to "consume" without knowing what happened in the kitchen. Often the equivalent of the "Consume" layer.
alias.Retail: A metaphorical layer name based on a Shop, representing curated, served data, ready for users to "consume" without knowing what happened in the warehouse, factory or storeroom. Often the equivalent of the "Consume" layer.
alias.Sandbox: A safe, isolated space where users can experiment with data without affecting production layers.
alias.Serving: A layer optimised for low-latency queries, API responses, or high-performance dashboarding. Often the equivalent of the "Consume" layer.
alias.Silver: Refined data that has been transformed and cleaned but may not yet be fully trusted or reconciled. Sits between Bronze and Gold. Often the equivalent of the "Design" layer.
alias.Snapshots: Point-in-time capture of data, typically aggregated data. Often the equivalent of the "Consume" layer.
alias.Staging: A temporary layer where data is held during data collection processing. Often the equivalent of the "Landing" layer.
alias.Stateful: Indicates that this layer maintains historical changes or data state over time, not just the latest version. Often the equivalent of the "History" layer.
alias.Storeroom: A place where data is stored but not necessarily cleaned or curated. Might be archived or infrequently used. Often the equivalent of the "History" layer.
alias.Tin: Sometimes used as a lightweight or experimental layer, lower trust or temporary data not ready for broader use.
alias.Trusted: Data that has been validated, reconciled, and approved for widespread business use. Often the equivalent of the "Consume" layer.

layer-governance-policy.Use of Layer Advised: Teams are ADVISED to move data through this layer, but it's not enforced. There's room for exceptions, especially in edge cases or innovation work.
layer-governance-policy.Use of Layer Enforced: Use of this layer is ENFORCED. The layer's purpose, structure, and use are mandated. Deviation is not permitted without formal approval.
layer-governance-policy.Use of Layer Optional: The layer is available for use, but entirely OPTIONAL. Teams can choose whether or not to use it depending on their specific needs. Often applies to experimental or transitional layers.

contains-pii.Yes: YES this layer holds PII Data.
contains-pii.No: NO this layer never holds PII Data.
contains-pii.Masked: This layer holds PII data, but it must be MASKED.

concept-modeling-patterns.Activity Schema: Data is conceptually designed based on the Activity Schema modeling pattern.
concept-modeling-patterns.AgileData: Data is conceptually designed based on the AgileData modeling pattern.
concept-modeling-patterns.Anchor: Data is conceptually designed based on the Anchor modeling pattern.
concept-modeling-patterns.BEAM: Data is conceptually designed based on the BEAM modeling pattern.
concept-modeling-patterns.Data Vault: Data is conceptually designed based on the Data Vault modeling pattern.
concept-modeling-patterns.Dimensional: Data is conceptually designed based on the Dimensional modeling pattern.
concept-modeling-patterns.ELM: Data is conceptually designed based on the ELM modeling pattern.
concept-modeling-patterns.Focal: Data is conceptually designed based on the Focal modeling pattern.
concept-modeling-patterns.Hook: Data is conceptually designed based on the Hook modeling pattern.
concept-modeling-patterns.Independent / Native Objects: Data is conceptually designed based on the Independent / Native Objects modeling pattern.
concept-modeling-patterns.One Attribute Set: Data is conceptually designed based on the One Attribute Set modeling pattern.
concept-modeling-patterns.One Big Table: Data is conceptually designed based on the One Big Table modeling pattern.
concept-modeling-patterns.Third Normal Form (3NF): Data is conceptually designed based on the Third Normal Form (3NF) modeling pattern.
concept-modeling-patterns.Unified Star Schema: Data is conceptually designed based on the Unified Star Schema modeling pattern.

data-persistence.Persisted: Data is PERSISTED in this layer.
data-persistence.Transient: Data is TRANSIENT, it is stored temporarily and deleted straight after it has been used.
data-persistence.Virtualised: Data is VIRTUALISED and never stored.
data-persistence.In-Memory: Data is stored IN-MEMORY but is not persistently stored in physical storage.
data-persistence.Other: Data is persisted in ANOTHER way.

data-storage-technology.AWS Redshift: Data is stored in AWS Redshift.
data-storage-technology.AWS S3: Data is stored in AWS S3.
data-storage-technology.Google BI Engine: Data is stored in Google BI Engine.
data-storage-technology.Google BigQuery: Data is stored in Google BigQuery.
data-storage-technology.Google Cloud Storage: Data is stored in Google Cloud Storage.
data-storage-technology.Microsoft Azure Blob Storage: Data is stored in Microsoft Azure Blob Storage.
data-storage-technology.Microsoft Azure Data Lake: Data is stored in Microsoft Azure Data Lake.
data-storage-technology.Microsoft Power BI: Data is stored in Microsoft Power BI.
data-storage-technology.Microsoft SQL Server: Data is stored in Microsoft SQL Server.
data-storage-technology.Microsoft SQL Server Analysis Services: Data is stored in Microsoft SQL Server Analysis Services.

data-storage-format.CSV: Data is stored in CSV format.
data-storage-format.Delta: Data is stored in Delta format.
data-storage-format.Hudi: Data is stored in Hudi format.
data-storage-format.Hyper: Data is stored in Hyper format.
data-storage-format.Iceberg: Data is stored in Iceberg format.
data-storage-format.JSON: Data is stored in JSON format.
data-storage-format.Native: Data is stored in Native format.
data-storage-format.Parquet: Data is stored in Parquet format.
data-storage-format.Vertipaq: Data is stored in Vertipaq format.

default-retention-period.0 (Transient): 0 - Transient or volatile logs, temporary caches, ephemeral processing data.
default-retention-period.24 hours: 24 Hours - Transient or volatile logs, temporary caches, ephemeral processing data.
default-retention-period.7 days: 7 Days - Short-term logs, debugging data, or staging data.
default-retention-period.30 days: 30 Days - Session data, audit logs, error reports, temporary backups.
default-retention-period.90 days (3 months): 90 Days - Usage analytics, system monitoring data, support tickets.
default-retention-period.180 days (6 months): 180 Days - Marketing attribution data, subscription trial histories.
default-retention-period.1 year: 1 Year - Transaction logs, system access records, non-identifiable event histories.
default-retention-period.3 years: 3 Years - Financial records, tax-related data, contracts, customer engagement history.
default-retention-period.7 years: 7 Years - Legal compliance (e.g. for financial services, healthcare, government).
default-retention-period.Indefinite (unless redacted): Indefinite - Typically for critical records, historical archives, or unless a deletion policy applies.

physical-data-structure.Activity Schema: Data is physically designed based on the Activity Schema modeling pattern.
physical-data-structure.Anchor: Data is physically designed based on the Anchor modeling pattern.
physical-data-structure.Data Vault: Data is physically designed based on the Data Vault modeling pattern.
physical-data-structure.Dimensional: Data is physically designed based on the Dimensional modeling pattern.
physical-data-structure.Focal: Data is physically designed based on the Focal modeling pattern.
physical-data-structure.Hook: Data is physically designed based on the Hook modeling pattern.
physical-data-structure.Independent Objects: Data is physically designed based on the Independent Objects modeling pattern.
physical-data-structure.Matches Source: Data is physically designed based on the Matches Source modeling pattern.
physical-data-structure.One Attribute Set: Data is physically designed based on the One Attribute Set modeling pattern.
physical-data-structure.One Big Tables: Data is physically designed based on the One Big Tables modeling pattern.
physical-data-structure.Other: Data is physically designed based on the Other modeling pattern.
physical-data-structure.Summary Tables: Data is physically designed based on the Summary Tables modeling pattern.
physical-data-structure.Third Normal Form (3NF): Data is physically designed based on the Third Normal Form (3NF) modeling pattern.
physical-data-structure.Unified Star Schema: Data is physically designed based on the Unified Star Schema modeling pattern.

source-driven.Yes: The data IS designed based on the source system data model.
source-driven.No: The data IS NOT designed based on the source system data model.

integrates-core-business-concepts.Yes: The layer DOES integrate data based on Core Business Concepts.
integrates-core-business-concepts.No: The layer does NOT integrate data based on Core Business Concepts.
integrates-core-business-concepts.Optional: Integration of data based on Core Business Concepts is OPTIONAL.

data-processing.Batch: Data is collected over a period of time and processed in large BATCHES at scheduled intervals.
data-processing.Micro Batch: Data is processed in SMALL, frequent BATCHES at short intervals.
data-processing.Streaming: Data is processed CONTINUOUSLY as it arrives.

change-detection.Change Data Capture (Database Logs): Changes are detected by reading the Inserts, Updates and Deletes in the DATABASE LOGS.
change-detection.Change Data Capture (Other): Another Change Detection Pattern is used.
change-detection.Snapshots: Changes are detected by comparing two SNAPSHOTS of the data and determining the differences.
change-detection.Streaming Inserts: Changes are STREAMED to the platform by another system.

destructive-rebuilds.Allowed: Destructive rebuild of data in this layer is ALLOWED.
destructive-rebuilds.Not Allowed: Destructive rebuild of data in this layer is NOT Allowed.
destructive-rebuilds.Allowed as an Exception: Destructive rebuild of data in this layer is ALLOWED as an EXCEPTION.

rename-fields.Any Reason: Fields can be Renamed for ANY REASON.
rename-fields.Driven by Business Context: Fields can be Renamed to align with BUSINESS CONTEXT.
rename-fields.Driven by Consumption: Fields can be Renamed to make it easier for last mile CONSUMPTION tools.
rename-fields.Driven by Standardisation: Fields can be Renamed to align with ORGANISATIONAL NAMING POLICIES.
rename-fields.Driven by Storage: Fields can be Renamed to meet the requirements of the STORAGE TECHNOLOGY.

data-value-updates.Allowed: Updates of data in this layer is ALLOWED.
data-value-updates.Not Allowed: Updates of data in this layer is NOT ALLOWED.
data-value-updates.Parsing Fields Only: Updates of data in this layer is ONLY ALLOWED when PARSING THE FIELDS value into multiple separate fields.

derived-data.Yes: Data CAN be Derived in this layer.
derived-data.No: Data CAN NOT be Derived in this layer.

calculate-metrics.No: Business Metrics are NOT calculated in this layer.
calculate-metrics.Facts: Facts ARE calculated in this layer.
calculate-metrics.Measures: Measures ARE calculated in this layer.
calculate-metrics.Metrics: Metrics ARE calculated in this layer.

auditable.No: NO the Layer is NOT Auditable.
auditable.Reconciles with Source: This layer can be reconciled to the SYSTEM OF CAPTURE.
auditable.Reconciles with Previous Layer: This layer can be reconciled to the PREVIOUS LAYER.

reconciliation-pattern.Compare Hash Records: Data is reconciled via Compare Hash Records.
reconciliation-pattern.Compare Row Count: Data is reconciled via Compare Row Count.
reconciliation-pattern.Full Data Compare: Data is reconciled via Full Data Compare.

data-contract-alerts.Yes: YES data contract alerts are issued for this layer.
data-contract-alerts.No: NO data contract alerts are not issued for this layer.

data-quality-alerts.Yes: YES data quality alerts are issued for this layer.
data-quality-alerts.No: NO data quality alerts are not issued for this layer.

persona-access-read.Analyst: Analyst Persona is ALLOWED to READ data in this layer.
persona-access-read.Analytics Engineer: Analytics Engineer Persona is ALLOWED to READ data in this layer.
persona-access-read.Data Engineer: Data Engineer Persona is ALLOWED to READ data in this layer.
persona-access-read.Data Scientist: Data Scientist Persona is ALLOWED to READ data in this layer.
persona-access-read.External: External Persona is ALLOWED to READ data in this layer.
persona-access-read.Information Consumer: Information Consumer Persona is ALLOWED to READ data in this layer.
persona-access-read.System Account: System Account Persona is ALLOWED to READ data in this layer.

persona-access-write.Analyst: Analyst Persona is ALLOWED to WRITE data into this layer.
persona-access-write.Analytics Engineer: Analytics Engineer Persona is ALLOWED to WRITE data into this layer.
persona-access-write.Data Engineer: Data Engineer Persona is ALLOWED to WRITE data into this layer.
persona-access-write.Data Scientist: Data Scientist Persona is ALLOWED to WRITE data into this layer.
persona-access-write.External: External Persona is ALLOWED to WRITE data into this layer.
persona-access-write.Information Consumer: Information Consumer Persona is ALLOWED to WRITE data into this layer.
persona-access-write.System Account: System Account Persona is ALLOWED to WRITE data into this layer.

last-mile-tools.Google BigQuery Console: Data is accessed using Google BigQuery Console.
last-mile-tools.Google Looker: Data is accessed using Google Looker.
last-mile-tools.Google Looker Studio: Data is accessed using Google Looker Studio.
last-mile-tools.Google Sheets: Data is accessed using Google Sheets.
last-mile-tools.Microsoft Excel: Data is accessed using Microsoft Excel.
last-mile-tools.Microsoft PowerBI: Data is accessed using Microsoft PowerBI.

history-window.All history: Every delivery carries the full history of the asset.
history-window.1 day: Each delivery contains the trailing 1 day of data.
history-window.3 days: Each delivery contains the trailing 3 days of data.
history-window.7 days: Each delivery contains the trailing 7 days of data.
history-window.30 days: Each delivery contains the trailing 30 days of data.
history-window.90 days: Each delivery contains the trailing 90 days of data.
history-window.1 year: Each delivery contains the trailing 1 year of data.

status.Ideation: An idea, not yet designed. Use while gathering requirements + sketching shape.
status.Design: Being drafted. Schema, rules, and SLAs are taking form but nothing is built yet.
status.Development: Being built. Pipelines, schemas, and rules are under active construction.
status.Testing: Being validated. Implementation exists but is being checked against the contract.
status.Production: Live. The contract is fulfilled; downstream consumers can rely on it.
status.Deprecated: Still available, but discouraged. New consumers should migrate to a replacement.
status.Retired: Removed from service. No longer published or supported.
